rulings.Case Title: DAVID BECK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS(B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 10, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Fischbach and Moore, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs¿ claims against it. Defendant¿s moving papers raised no issue other than the sophisticated user defense. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

Defendant must deliver a courtesy copy of these papers to Department 503 by January 10, 2013 at 4:00pm. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BECK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDENTS(B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 10, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and declaration would create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID WEDDING et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JANUARY 10, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 10, Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are continued to February 4, 2013. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is granted. Plaintiffs demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot, for reasons stated, be presented. Supplemental opposition, if any, due by January 23, 2013. Supplemental reply, if any, due by January 30, 2013. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. = (503/TLJ)
